prompt:
Quarterly access review again. The collector already pulls passwd/group-style snapshots off each host; I need the analysis half. One thing up front because a previous tool got it wrong: account and group names are CASE-SENSITIVE. 'Alice' listed in a group is not the account 'alice' — the last script treated them as the same and we shipped a wrong report.

Build `acctaudit.ps1` in the repo root: `pwsh -NoProfile -File acctaudit.ps1 -PasswdPath <file> -GroupPath <file> -AllowedShells <file>`.

Input formats: passwd rows are `name:x:uid:gid:gecos:home:shell` (exactly 7 fields, uid/gid numeric); group rows are `name:x:gid:members` (exactly 4 fields, gid numeric, members comma-separated — an empty members field means NO members, not one empty-named member). The shells file is one path per line; blank lines and `#` comments ignored. Any passwd/group row that breaks its format aborts: stderr `acctaudit: <leafname>: malformed line <n>` (1-based), exit 64, nothing on stdout.

The report is ONE JSON object on stdout (ConvertTo-Json, keys in exactly this order):

- orphanedMembers: {group, member} for every group member with no passwd account of that exact name; sorted ordinal by group then member.
- duplicateUids: {uid, users} for every uid held by more than one account; sorted by uid ascending NUMERICALLY (995 before 1002 even though '1002' < '995' as strings); users sorted ordinal.
- duplicateGids: same idea for group gids: {gid, groups}.
- shellViolations: {user, shell} for accounts whose shell is not in the allowlist; sorted ordinal by user.
- unknownPrimaryGroup: {user, gid} for accounts whose primary gid matches no group; sorted ordinal by user.

Empty findings are empty arrays, not missing keys. uid/gid are JSON numbers. Exit 65 when there is at least one finding of any class, 0 when all five arrays are empty.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_acctaudit.ps1` from the workspace root, unmodified — hands off the test file.
notes: 6P4 item 3: account audit over passwd/group fixtures, objects + ConvertTo-Json out, exit 0/65/64 contract. PowerShell-native teeth: default @{} hashtables and -contains are case-INSENSITIVE (OrdinalIgnoreCase), so the wheel member 'Alice' vs account 'alice' fixture forces an ordinal-comparer membership structure; duplicateUids 995-before-1002 forces numeric (not string) sort; 'legacy:x:50:' with empty members forces empty-split handling (''.Split(',') yields one empty element); byte-exact ConvertTo-Json compare pins [ordered] key order and empty arrays rendering as []. Fixture-driven sysadmin shape per wave law (no real /etc reads). Differentiation: bash-trayaudit/py-sched-audit audit different domains via text; no existing seed parses account databases; ps-assetdiff (6P3) diffs CSV snapshots — different input format, different findings model.
